Key Decision: Intercity (IC) is the clear winner тАФ direct service, First Class available, and no slow regional connections required.


Is It a Milk Run? First Class Options
Short answer: Take the Intercity (IC) train тАФ itтАЩs NOT a milk run and has First Class seating.

Why Intercity (IC) is the right choice: - Runs the full Naples тЖТ Brindisi route without changing trains - Makes major stops only (Foggia, Bari, coastal towns) тАФ not every tiny station - First Class on IC trains: reserved seat, more space, dedicated car, quieter - IC trains are not Frecciarossa-class but they are proper express trains - Fares: тВм15тАУ40 First Class depending on advance booking - Book at trenitalia.com тАФ search Naples Centrale тЖТ Brindisi, filter by IC
What makes the тАЬmilk runтАЭ concern valid: The BariтЖТBrindisi regional (Regionale) connection, if you take the Frecciarossa-then-change-at-Bari approach, IS slow тАФ it stops at many coastal towns. ThatтАЩs the connection to avoid. The IC bypasses this entirely.
Booking tip: IC trains must be booked on Trenitalia (Italo doesnтАЩt serve this southern route). Book 2тАУ4 weeks ahead for best fares and seat selection.


Understanding Puglia Geography
Important: Brindisi is IN Puglia, not before it. The train journey IS a tour through Puglia:
Route Path (North to South): 1. Caserta - Royal Palace (Reggia di Caserta), visible from station 2. Benevento - Roman Arch of Trajan 3. Foggia - Enter Puglia here (gateway to Gargano Peninsula) 4. Bari - PugliaтАЩs capital (~3.5h from Naples) 5. Polignano a Mare - Dramatic cliffside town 6. Ostuni - тАЬWhite CityтАЭ on hilltop 7. Brindisi - Historic port city (~5.5h from Naples) 8. Lecce - тАЬFlorence of the SouthтАЭ (~6h from Naples, 30 min beyond Brindisi)


Recommended Train
Intercity (IC) Naples Centrale тЖТ Brindisi - Morning departure recommended (arrive Brindisi early afternoon) - Check current schedules at trenitalia.com тАФ schedules change seasonally - First Class car: look for тАЬPrima ClasseтАЭ designation when booking - Journey time: approximately 5h 30min direct
Departure Times: - Morning Options: Earliest ~8:12 AM, Latest ~6:12 PM - Strategy: Take morning train to maximize daylight views through Puglia


Scenic Journey Highlights
What YouтАЩll See: - Crossing the Apennines (mountains) - Entering the Tavoliere delle Puglie (plains) - Adriatic coastline (sit on LEFT side after Foggia) - Olive groves stretching to horizon - Whitewashed towns on hillsides


Recommended Approach
Option A: Direct to Brindisi (Simple) - Morning Frecce train from Naples - Arrive Brindisi early afternoon (~5.5 hours) - Meet tour group or check into hotel - One journey, minimal hassle
Option B: Stop in Bari (Recommended if Time Allows) - Take morning train to Bari (3.5h) - Spend afternoon/evening in Bari Vecchia (old town) - Watch orecchiette ladies making pasta - Visit Basilica di San Nicola - Fresh seafood dinner along the port - Next morning: Easy 1-1.5 hour regional train to Brindisi
Bari Benefits: - Breaks up long journey - Taste of Puglia before tour begins - Beautiful old town (Bari Vecchia) - Day trip options: Matera (cave dwellings, 1h), Alberobello (trulli houses, 1.5h), Polignano a Mare (cliffs, 30 min)

title: Train Travel in Italy
text:
General Train Travel Tips

Booking Strategy
When to Book: - High-speed trains: 2-4 weeks ahead for Super Economy fares - Regional trains: Can buy same day (no reservations)
Where to Book: - Trenitalia.com or Italotreno.it (direct booking best) - Omio, Rail Europe (third-party, often higher fees) - Station ticket windows (if you prefer in-person)


At the Station
Arrival Time: - High-speed trains: Arrive 15-20 minutes early - Regional trains: Arrive 10 minutes early - Check platform (binario) on departure boards
Validating Tickets: - E-tickets (QR code): No validation needed, show on phone - Paper tickets: MUST validate in yellow machines before boarding - Failure to validate = fine from conductor


On the Train
Luggage: - Store large bags in designated racks (near doors or overhead) - Keep valuables with you - Watch bags during stops
Seats: - High-speed: Assigned seats (on ticket) - Regional: Open seating (first-come, first-served)
Quiet Cars: - Some trains have тАЬSilenzioтАЭ cars (no phone calls, quiet conversations)


Train Classes Compared
Trenitalia Classes: - Standard/2nd Class: Comfortable, affordable - Premium: More space, quieter - Business: Leather seats, welcome drink, snacks - Executive: Most spacious, full meal service (rare on our routes)
Italo Classes: - Smart: Economy - Comfort: Business equivalent - Prima: First class equivalent

title: Restaurant Recommendations
text:
General Italy Dining Tips

Meal Structure
Italian meals have courses: 1. Antipasto - Appetizer 2. Primo - First course (pasta, risotto, soup) 3. Secondo - Second course (meat or fish) 4. Contorno - Side vegetable (ordered separately) 5. Dolce - Dessert 6. Caff├и - Espresso
You donтАЩt have to order everything! Common to order: - Primo + contorno - OR Secondo + contorno - OR Antipasto + primo


Ordering
Cover Charge (Coperto): тВм1-3 per person, for bread and table service Service Charge: Sometimes included (servizio incluso) - check bill Water: Ask for тАЬacqua naturaleтАЭ (still) or тАЬfrizzanteтАЭ (sparkling) Wine: House wine (vino della casa) often excellent and cheap


Tipping

Not expected like in U.S.
Round up bill or leave 5-10% for excellent service
Never 15-20%



Coffee Culture
Stand at the bar: Cheapest (тВм1-1.50 for espresso) Sit at table: 2-3x more expensive
Types: - Espresso (caff├и) - Standard coffee - Cappuccino - Only acceptable before 11 AM - Caff├и macchiato - Espresso with dash of milk - Caff├и americano - Espresso with hot water (watered-down to Americans)
Never order: Cappuccino after a meal (Italians will judge)

title: Packing List
text:
Luggage Strategy

Checked Bag
1 checked bag per person (max 50 lbs/23 kg for economy)
What Goes Here: - Most clothes - Toiletries over 3.4 oz - Shoes (except what youтАЩre wearing) - Souvenirs on return


Carry-On
1 carry-on + 1 personal item
Carry-On Bag Contains: - 1-2 changes of clothes (in case checked bag delayed) - Medications - Valuables (jewelry, electronics) - Travel documents - Phone chargers - Essentials for first 24 hours
Personal Item (Backpack/Purse): - Passport, wallet, phone - Snacks, water bottle (empty through security) - Book, headphones - Sweater/light jacket

title: Packing List
text:
Footwear (Most Important!)

Walking Shoes (Pack 2 Pairs)
Priority #1: YouтАЩll walk 5-10 miles per day on cobblestones
Option 1: Broken-in sneakers or walking shoes - MUST be already broken in (no blisters!) - Good arch support - Cushioned sole - Examples: Allbirds, Hoka, Brooks, New Balance
Option 2: Comfortable sandals with support - NOT flip-flops (no support) - Closed-toe for cobblestones - Examples: Birkenstocks, Teva, Keen
Rule: Alternate shoes daily (give feet/shoes rest)


Nicer Shoes (1 Pair)

Comfortable flats or low heels for dinners
MUST still be walkable (youтАЩll walk to restaurant)
Avoid stilettos on cobblestones

Pro Tip: Break in ALL shoes 2 months before trip!

title: Naples Overview
text:
Napoli - Raw, chaotic, deeply cultured, and home to some of the worldтАЩs finest archaeology and the best pizza on Earth. Southern ItalyтАЩs largest city is gritty, loud, and utterly authentic - a place that doesnтАЩt try to please tourists but rewards those who embrace its energy.
Our Time Here: 2 full days (June 11-12) + arrival afternoon (June 10)
Why Naples? - Gateway to Herculaneum (better than Pompeii) - World-class archaeological museum (MANN) - Birthplace of pizza - Authentic southern Italian culture - Dramatic setting on the Bay of Naples with Vesuvius backdrop

title: Naples Overview
text:
Napoli - Raw, chaotic, deeply cultured, and home to some of the worldтАЩs finest archaeology and the best pizza on Earth. Southern ItalyтАЩs largest city is gritty, loud, and utterly authentic - a place that doesnтАЩt try to please tourists but rewards those who embrace its energy.
Our Time Here: 2 full days (June 11-12) + arrival afternoon (June 10)
Why Naples? - Gateway to Herculaneum (better than Pompeii) - World-class archaeological museum (MANN) - Birthplace of pizza - Authentic southern Italian culture - Dramatic setting on the Bay of Naples with Vesuvius backdrop

title: Naples Overview
text:
Trip Context

Arrival (June 10)
High-speed train from Florence (~2.5 hours), arriving early afternoon. Check into hotel, orientation walk, first taste of Neapolitan pizza.


Full Days (June 11-12)
Day 1 (June 11): Herculaneum guided tour (morning/midday), MANN museum or Cappella Sansevero, evening in historic center
Day 2 (June 12): MANN museum (if not done yesterday), Spaccanapoli district, Cappella Sansevero, waterfront walk, final Naples evening


Departure (June 13)
Morning checkout, train to Puglia (Brindisi) - scenic 5-6 hour journey through southern Italy.

title: Naples Overview
text:
Naples at a Glance

The Authentic South
Naples is everything Florence isnтАЩt: chaotic instead of refined, gritty instead of polished, loud instead of reserved. But itтАЩs deeply rewarding:

History: Greek colony (8th century BC), major Roman city, capital of Kingdom of Naples
Culture: Opera (invented here), commedia dellтАЩarte, Neapolitan song
Archaeology: Guarding the treasures of Pompeii and Herculaneum
Food: Pizza Margherita was invented here (1889)
Character: Napoletani are passionate, expressive, and proud

Fair Warning: Naples has rough edges. ItтАЩs not dangerous, but it requires awareness and an open mind. The rewards - incredible food, world-class art, genuine Italian life - far outweigh any initial culture shock.

title: Naples Overview
text:
Major Attractions

Archaeological Sites (Our Priority)
Herculaneum тнР TOP PRIORITY - Better preserved than Pompeii (sealed by volcanic mud, not ash) - Upper floors, wooden furniture, intact frescoes - The Boat Houses: 300 skeletons visible, most powerful archaeological moment in Italy - 20 minutes by Circumvesuviana train from Naples - Book guided tour in advance (essential for understanding)
Mount Vesuvius (Optional) - The volcano that destroyed Pompeii and Herculaneum (AD 79) - Hike to crater rim (~30 min uphill) - Incredible Bay of Naples views - Can combine with Herculaneum tour - Still active (last eruption 1944)


Museums
MANN - Museo Archeologico Nazionale тнР ESSENTIAL - WorldтАЩs finest collection of Roman artifacts - Most treasures from Pompeii & Herculaneum - Alexander Mosaic (from House of the Faun, Pompeii) - Farnese Collection (massive Roman sculptures) - Secret Cabinet (Roman erotic art) - Egyptian collection - Visit AFTER Herculaneum for context
Cappella Sansevero тнР MUST SEE - Giuseppe SanmartinoтАЩs Veiled Christ (1753) - Marble veil appears translucent (one of worldтАЩs most astonishing sculptures) - Tiny chapel - book timed entry ahead - 30-45 minutes, unforgettable - No photography allowed


Churches & Historic Sites
Duomo di Napoli (Cathedral) - Chapel of San Gennaro (NaplesтАЩ patron saint) - His blood тАЬliquefiesтАЭ 3 times a year (miraculous) - Baroque architecture
Castel Nuovo (Maschio Angioino) - Medieval castle - Civic Museum - Views of harbor
Napoli Sotterranea (Underground Naples) - 40 meters below street level - Greek aqueducts, Roman theater, WWII shelters - Guided tours only

title: Naples Overview
text:
Neighborhoods

Centro Storico (Historic Center)
Spaccanapoli - Arrow-straight street splitting old Naples - Follows ancient Greek city plan (2,400 years old!) - Churches, shops, street life - Via dei Tribunali parallel street (pizza central)
What to See: - Chiesa del Ges├╣ Nuovo (Baroque church) - Santa Chiara (monastery, beautiful cloister) - San Domenico Maggiore (Gothic church) - Street vendors, nativity workshops, local life
Character: Chaotic, loud, hanging laundry, narrow streets, Vespas everywhere - authentic Naples.


Quartieri Spagnoli (Spanish Quarters)

Grid of narrow streets west of Spaccanapoli
Working-class neighborhood
Maradona murals (heтАЩs a god here)
Excellent cheap food
Not touristy

Safety Note: Fine during day, avoid at night.


Chiaia & Vomero
Chiaia: - Upscale shopping district - Via Chiaia (shopping street) - Safer, cleaner than centro storico - Good hotels and restaurants
Vomero: - Hilltop neighborhood - Residential, quieter - Castel SantтАЩElmo (fortress with city views) - Certosa di San Martino (monastery museum) - Funicular from below


Lungomare (Waterfront)

Via Partenope and Via Caracciolo
Promenade along Bay of Naples
Vesuvius views
Castel dellтАЩOvo (seaside castle)
Excellent seafood restaurants
Cleaner, prettier than centro

Recommended for: Sunset walks, jogging, escaping chaos

title: Naples Overview
text:
Getting Around

Metro
Two Lines: - Line 1 (Orange): Connects Vomero, Centro Storico, station - Line 2 (Blue): Connects Napoli Centrale to Pozzuoli
Tickets: тВм1.10 for 100 minutes Art Stations: Some stations are museums (Toledo, Universit├а stunning)


Circumvesuviana
Commuter Rail: - Lower level of Napoli Centrale (Garibaldi) station - Goes to: Herculaneum (Ercolano Scavi), Pompeii, Sorrento - тВм2.80 to Herculaneum - Old trains, watch belongings (pickpockets)


Funicular
Three funiculars to Vomero: - Montesanto, Centrale, Chiaia - тВм1.10 tickets (same as metro) - Scenic ride up hill


Walking
Centro Storico walkable but: - Chaotic traffic (cars, Vespas, pedestrians all mixed) - Narrow sidewalks or none - Watch for Vespas on sidewalks (yes, really) - Not as pedestrian-friendly as Florence
Pro Tip: Cross streets with confidence (hesitation is dangerous).


Taxis

White cars with тАЬTAXIтАЭ sign and meter
Use official taxis only (avoid touts)
Set fare from airport (~тВм20-25 to center)
Apps: Free Now, it Taxi

Scam Alert: Always insist on meter or agree on price before starting.

title: Naples Overview
text:
Food & Wine

Pizza (The Whole Reason to Visit)
Naples invented pizza. This is where itтАЩs best.
Classic Napoletana: - Thin, soft, charred crust - San Marzano tomatoes - Buffalo mozzarella - Basil - Wood-fired oven (900┬░F, 90 seconds)
Must-Try Pizzerias:
Da Michele (Since 1870) - Only 2 pizzas: Marinara or Margherita - тВм5-7, cash only - Expect lines - Pure, simple, perfect
Sorbillo (Via dei Tribunali) - More variety - Always packed - Reservations possible for dinner
50 Kal├▓ (Modern, upscale) - Gourmet pizza - Higher prices (тВм12-18) - Reservation essential
Rule: If you only eat one meal in Naples, make it pizza.


Other Neapolitan Specialties
Pasta: - Spaghetti alle vongole (with clams) - Pasta alla Genovese (onion rag├╣, no basil despite name)
Seafood: - Fritto misto (mixed fried seafood) - Polpo (octopus) - Fresh fish (all from Bay of Naples or nearby waters)
Street Food: - Sfogliatella - Shell-shaped pastry (ricotta filling) - Baba al rhum - Rum-soaked sponge cake - Pizza fritta - Fried pizza (filled, folded, deep-fried) - Cuoppo - Fried seafood in paper cone
Coffee: - Espresso culture serious here - тВм1 at bar (standing) - Never cappuccino after 11 AM


Where to Eat
See: Restaurants page for specific recommendations.
General Tips: - Eat pizza on Via dei Tribunali - Seafood on Lungomare - Cheap local food in Quartieri Spagnoli - Skip tourist traps near port

title: Naples Overview
text:
Practical Information

When WeтАЩre There
June 10-12: Early summer - Weather: High 80s-90┬░F (30-32┬░C), humid - Crowds: Moderate (fewer tourists than Florence) - Sunset: ~8:30 PM


Safety
Naples has a reputation but itтАЩs largely outdated:
Actual Risks: - Pickpockets: Watch bags in crowded areas, on metro, Circumvesuviana - Bag snatching: Keep purses zipped, crossbody style - Scams: Restaurant touts, overpriced taxis, fake tour guides
NOT a Risk: - Violent crime against tourists (extremely rare) - Wandering during day (perfectly safe)
Be Smart: - DonтАЩt flash expensive jewelry, cameras - Keep valuables close in crowds - Use hotel safe for passports - Stay aware of surroundings - Trust instincts
Neighborhoods to Avoid at Night: - Quartieri Spagnoli (okay during day) - Immediately around Napoli Centrale station - Industrial areas
Safe at Night: - Chiaia - Vomero - Lungomare - Parts of centro storico (stick to main streets)


Money
Budget Per Day (Per Person): - Lodging: тВм60-100 (shared room) - Food: тВм25-40 (pizza is cheap!) - Museums/tours: тВм20-80 (Herculaneum tour is biggest expense) - Transport: тВм5-10 - Total: тВм110-230 per day
Cheaper than Florence!


Useful Phrases
Neapolitan Dialect different from standard Italian:

Buongiorno тЖТ тАЬBuonтАЩ jornтАЭ
Grazie тЖТ тАЬGrazzieтАЭ (strong Z)
Come sta? тЖТ тАЬCommтАЩ sta?тАЭ

TheyтАЩll appreciate any attempt at Italian (or Neapolitan).

title: Naples Overview
text:
Tips for Naples

Embrace the Chaos
Naples is overwhelming at first: - Traffic is insane - Streets are loud - Everything feels disorganized
But thatтАЩs the point. Naples doesnтАЩt perform for tourists. ItтАЩs authentically itself - take it or leave it. Once you stop expecting FlorenceтАЩs refinement, youтАЩll love NaplesтАЩ raw energy.


DonтАЩt Compare to Florence
Different cities, different vibes: - Florence: Museum city, refined, Renaissance - Naples: Living city, gritty, authentic
Both are incredible. Judge Naples on its own terms.


The Herculaneum Decision
Most tourists go to Pompeii. WeтАЩre going to Herculaneum because: - Better preserved (organic materials intact) - Smaller, more manageable (not overwhelming) - Fewer crowds - More emotionally powerful (the Boat Houses) - Closer to Naples (20 min vs.┬а40 min)
If you have time for only one: Herculaneum > Pompeii.


MANN Museum Timing
Go AFTER Herculaneum: - Seeing the ruins first gives context - Artifacts make more sense - YouтАЩll appreciate what youтАЩre looking at
Not before - itтАЩs backwards chronologically.

title: Naples Overview
section: Our Naples Itinerary at a Glance
text:

title: Day 10 тАФ June 14: First Full Day in Puglia
section: Morning: Brindisi on Foot (9 AMтАУ1 PM)
text:
Morning: Brindisi on Foot (9 AMтАУ1 PM)
After the intensity of Florence and Naples, Brindisi is refreshingly calm. A working Mediterranean port, not a tourist hotspot тАФ which is exactly the point.

The Waterfront & Historic Center
Start: Coffee at a local bar near the hotel (not a tourist caf├й тАФ ask the hotel where the locals go).
Walk the inner harbor (Seno di Ponente):







Site
What It Is




Colonne Romane
One intact Roman column marking the end of the Appian Way (Via Appia). This is where ancient Rome physically ended тАФ Julius Caesar, Cicero, Virgil all passed through.


Seno di Ponente
The sheltered inner harbor тАФ fishing boats, Mediterranean ferries, unhurried pace


Castello Svevo
Norman-era castle on the harbor; exterior worth seeing


Piazza Vittorio Emanuele
Main square near the waterfront


Corso Garibaldi
Pedestrian main street into centro storico



Walk time: 60тАУ90 minutes, relaxed pace.


Museo Archeologico Nazionale di Brindisi (Optional)

Modest museum with finds from the ancient Greek and Roman city
Free or low cost entry; helpful context for the columns and the portтАЩs 2,500-year history
Located near the waterfront
crumbs: Day 10 тАФ June 14: First Full Day in Puglia

title: Day 10 тАФ June 14: First Full Day in Puglia
text:
Afternoon: Rest & Puglia Context (1тАУ5 PM)
This is a recovery afternoon. YouтАЩve spent a week in Florence and nearly four days in Naples. Puglia is beautiful but itтАЩs also six more days of exploration. Spend this afternoon reading, resting, or strolling lightly.
Use the time for: - [ ] Read Puglia Overview to understand what youтАЩll be visiting - [ ] Review tour details and confirm pickup times/meeting points - [ ] Check accommodations for the coming days - [ ] Get cash if needed тАФ ATMs are easy to find in centro - [ ] A leisurely lunch near the harbor
Light option: Walk to the Tempio di San Giovanni al Sepolcro (a small round Crusader church from the 11th century тАФ easy to miss, worth finding).
crumbs: Day 10 тАФ June 14: First Full Day in Puglia

title: Day 10 тАФ June 14: First Full Day in Puglia
text:
Evening: Dinner in Puglia (7тАУ9 PM)
You started getting acquainted with Puglian food last night. Tonight, go deeper.
PugliaтАЩs table:







Dish
What to Know




Orecchiette alle cime di rapa
The definitive Puglian pasta тАФ тАЬlittle earsтАЭ with bitter turnip greens and anchovy. Get it if itтАЩs on the menu.


Burrata
If you havenтАЩt had it fresh in Puglia yet, this is the night. Born in the region тАФ nothing like what you get abroad.


Fave e cicorie
Fava bean pur├йe with bitter greens тАФ ancient peasant dish, stunning


Tiella di riso, patate e cozze
Layered bake of rice, potatoes, and mussels тАФ a Bari/Puglia classic


Primitivo
Fuller-bodied red from Manduria тАФ donтАЩt skip it



Seafood: Brindisi is a fishing port. Whatever the restaurant says is fresh today, get that.
Where to look: - Side streets off Corso Garibaldi heading into centro storico - Avoid restaurants with multilingual photo menus on the waterfront - Ask the hotel for a specific recommendation тАФ they know

title: Day 8 тАФ June 12: Naples тАФ Herculaneum & Vesuvius
text:
Morning/Day: Herculaneum & Vesuvius Tour
Check your tour confirmation for the exact schedule. A typical Herculaneum + Vesuvius day:
Herculaneum ruins (2тАУ3 hours)
Herculaneum was buried under a 20-meter lava flow when Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD тАФ which preserved far more than PompeiiтАЩs ash burial.







Site
Why It Matters




The Boat Houses
300 skeletons of people who fled to the shore тАФ the most emotionally powerful moment on the site


House of Neptune & Amphitrite
Vivid blue-and-gold mosaics, astonishingly preserved


Casa dei Cervi
Elite villa with original frescoes and garden statues


Thermopolium
Ancient fast-food counter with food residue still identifiable


Wooden structures
Actual original wood тАФ doors, furniture, beams тАФ impossible at Pompeii



Mount Vesuvius (1тАУ1.5 hours)
Drive up the volcano, then hike ~30 minutes to the crater rim. Looking down into the crater and back across the bay to Naples тАФ this is the source of the destruction you just walked through.

Wear sturdy shoes тАФ the path is volcanic gravel
Bring water and layers (wind at the top)
Views of Naples, the bay, and Capri on clear days
crumbs: Day 8 тАФ June 12: Naples тАФ Herculaneum & Vesuvius

MANN Museum тАФ Now that youтАЩve seen Herculaneum, the objects have context. тВм18; 2тАУ3 hours; metro to Museo stop.
Rest тАФ A long day in the ruins and on the volcano earns a rest.

title: Day 6 тАФ June 10: Florence to Naples
text:
Arrive Naples (3:28pm)

Napoli Centrale Station
YouтАЩll emerge from a sleek high-speed train into one of ItalyтАЩs most chaotic stations. This is normal тАФ Naples is different.
After arriving:

Follow тАЬUscitaтАЭ (exit) signs toward Piazza Garibaldi
Watch your belongings тАФ the station and piazza are busy pickpocket zones; keep bags zipped and phones in pockets
Get to the taxi stand immediately outside and take an official white taxi тАФ agree on price or insist on the meter (~тВм15тАУ20 to most central hotels)
Avoid anyone offering тАЬtaxiтАЭ on the concourse тАФ use only the official stand



Airbnb Check-In тАФ Casa Wenner (~4:00pm)
Casa Wenner Large тАФ Naples Center Chiaia Plebiscito Via Monte di Dio, 66, Naples 80132 Host: Pierfrancesco (+39 335 607 1111) | Antonella (+39 320 118 6998) Confirmation: HMTBH3HCCX | Check-in: 3:00 PM






NoteTiming Note



The train arrives Napoli Centrale at 3:28 PM. Taxi from the station to Via Monte di Dio takes 15тАУ20 minutes. Plan on arriving at the Airbnb around 3:45тАУ4:00 PM тАФ well after the 3:00 PM check-in opens, no problem.



Drop bags, freshen up, breathe
Contact Pierfrancesco or Antonella if any check-in questions
Note: Naples is louder and more chaotic than Florence. ThatтАЩs the charm тАФ lean into it.

title: Day 6 тАФ June 10: Florence to Naples
section: Evening: First Night in Naples (7тАУ10 PM)
text:
Evening: First Night in Naples (7тАУ10 PM)
Pizza is the only correct choice for tonight.
Naples invented pizza. Specifically, the Margherita was created here in 1889. Tonight you honor that.
See: Restaurants тАФ Naples Pizza
Top options:

Da Michele тАФ Legendary, cash only, expect a line, worth it. Only two pizzas: Margherita or Marinara.
Sorbillo тАФ Via dei Tribunali, excellent, often crowded
50 Kal├▓ тАФ More upscale, worth reserving ahead

What to order: - Margherita (tomato, mozzarella, basil) тАФ the original, invented here - Marinara (tomato, garlic, oregano, no cheese) тАФ older than the Margherita, equally essential - Cost: тВм5тАУ12 depending on place
After pizza: - Sfogliatella тАФ shell-shaped ricotta pastry, a Naples specialty тАФ grab one from a pasticceria - Short evening walk toward Spaccanapoli or the waterfront - Early to bed: the walking tour pickup is 8:30 AM

title: Day 4 тАФ June 8: Florence тАФ Duomo Complex
section: Morning: Santa Reparata тАФ Timed Entry (10:30 AM SHARP)
text:
Morning: Santa Reparata тАФ Timed Entry (10:30 AM SHARP)






ImportantTimed Entry тАФ Cannot Be Changed



Santa Reparata: 10:30 AM тАФ Porta Campanile entrance
This is a fixed timed ticket that cannot be rescheduled. Arrive by 10:20 AM. Drop any backpacks at luggage storage (Piazza Duomo 38/r) before arriving at the entrance.



What Is Santa Reparata?
The crypt beneath the Duomo floor тАФ the original 4th-century cathedral on which the current Duomo was built. Excavations reveal layer upon layer of Florentine history: Roman remains, the medieval church, BrunelleschiтАЩs tomb.







Element
What to Know




BrunelleschiтАЩs Tomb
The architect who built the Dome is buried here тАФ simple, almost anonymous slab


Roman remains
Sections of Roman-era structure visible in the foundations


Medieval mosaics
Fragments of the original floor, remarkably preserved


Depth of history
Walking over 2,000 years of layers beneath the cathedral floor



Time: Allow 45тАУ60 minutes.
crumbs: Day 4 тАФ June 8: Florence тАФ Duomo Complex

title: Day 4 тАФ June 8: Florence тАФ Duomo Complex
section: Also Valid on Your Ghiberti Pass
text:
Also Valid on Your Ghiberti Pass
Your pass (87-MKTRBNH) is valid June 8тАУ10 for these components тАФ visit any that fit around the 10:30 timed entry:

Baptistery of San Giovanni тАФ North Door Entrance
The octagonal baptistery across from the Duomo facade. Lorenzo GhibertiтАЩs bronze doors (the ones that gave this pass its name) are on the North and East sides.







Door
What It Is




East Doors (Gates of Paradise)
GhibertiтАЩs masterpiece тАФ Michelangelo called them worthy of paradise. What you see are high-quality reproductions; originals in the museum.


North Doors
GhibertiтАЩs earlier work тАФ still extraordinary; your pass enters here



Time: 30тАУ45 minutes inside.


Opera del Duomo Museum (Museo dellтАЩOpera)
Piazza del Duomo 9 тАФ the museum housing the actual originals removed from the Duomo exterior for preservation.







Highlight
What to See




GhibertiтАЩs Gates of Paradise (originals)
The actual bronze panels тАФ extraordinary up close


MichelangeloтАЩs Bandini Piet├а
Late Michelangelo, unfinished, raw, extraordinary тАФ carved for his own tomb


DonatelloтАЩs Mary Magdalene
Haunting carved wood figure; one of his most powerful works


Luca della RobbiaтАЩs Cantoria
Marble choir gallery with children singing and dancing


ArnolfoтАЩs Madonna
13th-century sculpture from the original cathedral facade



Time: 1тАУ1.5 hours. This museum is excellent and undervisited тАФ give it time.

title: Day 2 тАФ June 6: Frankfurt to Florence
text:
Arrival in Frankfurt (2:05pm)

Landing at Frankfurt Airport (FRA) Terminal 1
After 8.5 hours in the air, you arrive in Frankfurt тАФ one of EuropeтАЩs largest transit hubs. No need to exit the airport or clear full customs (youтАЩre in transit to Florence).
After deplaning: 1. Follow signs for Connections / Anschlussfl├╝ge (do not follow signs for тАЬArrivals/AusgangтАЭ) 2. If needed, clear Schengen entry passport control (for transit within EU, often not required тАФ follow agent instructions) 3. Check departure boards for LH 9498 gate 4. Terminal 1 is large тАФ allow 30тАУ45 minutes to reach your gate


Frankfurt Layover (2:05pmтАУ4:50pm) тАФ ~2 Hours 45 Minutes
This is a comfortable connection тАФ but Frankfurt is large. Stay efficient.

Find LH 9498 departure gate on boards
Get coffee and a snack тАФ there are excellent options throughout T1
Use the bathroom and freshen up
Be at gate by 4:20pm (30 min before departure)

If you feel fresh enough: Terminal 1 has a small duty-free area and shops тАФ good place to pick up a Frankfurt airport magnet.

About the Embraer 195

title: Day 2 тАФ June 6: Frankfurt to Florence
text:
Arrival: Florence (6:30pm)

Florence Airport тАФ Amerigo Vespucci Airport (FLR)
FlorenceтАЩs airport is small and easy. YouтАЩll be out quickly.
After landing: 1. Passport/EU entry control тАФ Have passport ready. If you didnтАЩt clear Schengen at Frankfurt, youтАЩll clear it here. 2. Baggage claim тАФ Collect both checked bags. Small airport = bags arrive quickly (~15тАУ20 min) 3. Customs тАФ Walk through тАЬNothing to DeclareтАЭ (green lane) 4. Arrivals hall тАФ YouтАЩre in Italy!


Getting to Your Hotel (~7:00тАУ7:30pm)
Option A: Taxi (Recommended) - Official taxi stand immediately outside arrivals - Fixed fare to city center: тВм22тАУ25 - ~20 minutes to most Florence hotels - No app needed тАФ just queue at the white taxis
Option B: Tram (T2 Line) - FlorenceтАЩs new airport tram (T2) connects FLR to the city center - Cost: ~тВм1.50, every 8тАУ10 minutes - ~25 minutes to SMN (Santa Maria Novella station area) - Good option if hotel is near the historic center and you have manageable luggage


Hotel Check-In (~7:00тАУ8:00pm)

Most hotels hold reservations until midnight тАФ no rush
Ask for luggage storage if checking in early in future
Get a city map from the front desk
Ask about breakfast times and hours
crumbs: Day 2 тАФ June 6: Frankfurt to Florence

title: Day 2 тАФ June 6: Frankfurt to Florence
text:
Evening: First Night in Florence

Managing Arrival Energy
YouтАЩve been traveling for roughly 24 hours (since leaving home yesterday). YouтАЩll be tired but probably buzzing with excitement.
The right approach: - DonтАЩt try to see the Uffizi tonight - A short walk to orient yourself is perfect - Find a restaurant within walking distance of the hotel - Eat a proper meal and drink water - Be in bed by 10pm Italian time to start resetting your clock


First Florence Walk (30тАУ45 minutes)
After dropping bags and freshening up, take a short evening walk: - Find the Arno River тАФ just follow it, youтАЩll know youтАЩre in Florence - Cross one of the bridges if youтАЩre close (Ponte Vecchio is the famous one) - Get gelato (your first in Italy тАФ important) - Just soak in being here


Dinner

Pick something close to the hotel тАФ tonight is not the night for a destination restaurant
Look for a simple trattoria with pasta and house wine
Avoid the tourist traps immediately adjacent to major sights (overpriced, worse food)
Ask the hotel front desk for a nearby recommendation

Flight 2: Chicago тЖТ Frankfurt (LH 0433)
Aircraft: Airbus A343 (wide-body long-haul jet) Duration: 8 hours 35 minutes Cabin: Premium Economy тАФ wider seats, more legroom, dedicated service Meals: Refreshments + full meal service

Departs ORD Terminal 1, 10:30pm (Friday, June 5)
Arrives FRA Terminal 1, 2:05pm (Saturday, June 6)


Premium Economy on Lufthansa A343
What to expect: - Dedicated Premium Economy cabin (rows 20тАУ30 typically) - Seat width: ~18тАУ19 inches, seat pitch: ~38 inches - Adjustable footrest, headrest, lumbar support - Premium meal service with real crockery - Dedicated overhead bin space - Priority boarding after Business class


Sleep Strategy (Transatlantic)
Florence is UTC+2 in June (7 hours ahead of Madison CDT). To minimize jet lag: - Set your watch to Italian time immediately at boarding (~17:30 Italian time when you board) - Flight arrives Frankfurt at 2:05pm Italian time тАФ try to sleep 7+ hours on board - Decline meals if they interfere with sleep - Use eye mask and earplugs - Recline seat fully, use footrest


In-Flight Tips
Do: - Hydrate steadily (water, avoid excessive alcohol) - Walk aisles every 2 hours (compression socks help) - Sleep on an Italian schedule
DonтАЩt: - Overdrink alcohol (dehydrates, worsens jet lag) - Stay seated the entire flight

Morning

WhatтАЩs Included in Combo Ticket (тВм30): - Cathedral nave (free, but combo ticket needed for other sites) - BrunelleschiтАЩs Dome climb тнР MUST DO - GiottoтАЩs Bell Tower (Campanile) - Baptistery - Opera Museum - Crypt
Priority Order:
1. Dome Climb (463 steps) - FIRST - Timed entry required - check your booking time - Go early before heat of day - No elevator, narrow passages (not for claustrophobic) - Incredible views of Florence from top - See VasariтАЩs Last Judgment frescoes up close - Allow 45-60 minutes
2. Cathedral Interior - Free entry to nave - Massive scale, relatively simple interior - Look up at dome from inside - 20-30 minutes
3. Baptistery - GhibertiтАЩs тАЬGates of ParadiseтАЭ bronze doors - Byzantine mosaics in dome - 20 minutes
4. Bell Tower (Optional) - 414 steps, different views than dome - Skip if tired from dome climb

title: Day 5 тАФ June 9: Florence тАФ Uffizi & Accademia
text:
Morning: Private Tour (9:00 AMтАУ1 PM)






ImportantMeeting Point тАФ 9:00 AM Sharp



Via dei Castellani, 14, 50122 Firenze FI
This is the street running along the east side of the Uffizi, facing the Arno. Look for your guide at the entrance. Arrive by 8:50 AM.
Cancellation deadline: 9:00 AM Central European Summer Time (the day before).



What YouтАЩll See
Uffizi Gallery
The worldтАЩs finest collection of Italian Renaissance painting, housed in the building Vasari designed for the Medici.








Work
Artist
Why It Matters




Birth of Venus
Botticelli
The defining image of Renaissance humanism


Primavera
Botticelli
Mythological allegory; 500+ plant species identifiable


Annunciation
Leonardo da Vinci
One of his earliest works; remarkable spatial depth


Doni Tondo
Michelangelo
His only finished panel painting; precursor to the Sistine ceiling


Duke and Duchess of Urbino
Piero della Francesca
Dual portraits; landscapes are the first true Italian landscapes


Venus of Urbino
Titian
Benchmark for every reclining nude that followed



A private guide makes the Uffizi genuinely comprehensible тАФ the collection is enormous and context transforms everything.
Galleria dellтАЩAccademia

MichelangeloтАЩs David тАФ 17 feet of marble, carved when Michelangelo was 26. Stand in front and walk around it. The Prisoners (unfinished figures in the corridor) show his process: figures emerging from stone.
Musical instrument collection (Stradivari violins, etc.)

Evening: First Night in Puglia (7:30тАУ10 PM)
Welcome to the heel of the boot. The cuisine changes immediately тАФ youтАЩre no longer in Naples.
Puglia staples that start tonight:







Dish
Description




Orecchiette
тАЬLittle earsтАЭ pasta, often with turnip greens (cime di rapa) or rag├╣


Burrata
Fresh mozzarella filled with cream тАФ PugliaтАЩs most famous gift

Fava bean pur├йe with bitter greens тАФ ancient, extraordinary


Primitivo
Local red wine тАФ rich and full-bodied, from Manduria

Another Puglian red тАФ darker, more tannic



Where to eat: Walk toward the inner harbor and look for family-run trattorie on Corso Garibaldi or the streets into centro storico. Avoid anything immediately adjacent to the tourist waterfront.

Optional: Walk the Inner Harbor
BrindisiтАЩs waterfront is compact and walkable тАФ even after a travel day:

Colonne Romane тАФ Two Roman columns (one intact) marking the end of the Appian Way; where ancient Rome ended. Julius Caesar, Cicero, Augustus walked here.
Seno di Ponente тАФ The sheltered inner harbor; fishing boats and Mediterranean ferries
Castello Svevo тАФ Norman-era castle overlooking the harbor







NoteEasy Does It Tonight



YouтАЩve done two flights and spent a week each in Florence and Naples. Tonight is dinner, a short walk, and sleep. Puglia starts tomorrow.

title: Italy Trip 2026
text:
Why Italy? Why These Regions?
Italy offers layers of history that few places can match. Florence gave birth to the Renaissance, preserving centuries of artistic genius in its museums and churches. Naples guards some of the worldтАЩs finest archaeological treasures from Pompeii and Herculaneum. And PugliaтАФItalyтАЩs hidden gemтАФbeckons with its unique trulli houses, baroque architecture, and authentic southern Italian culture largely untouched by mass tourism.
This trip combines three distinct Italian experiences: - Florence: Four days immersed in Renaissance artтАФMichelangeloтАЩs David, BotticelliтАЩs Birth of Venus, BrunelleschiтАЩs Dome, and countless treasures from humanityтАЩs creative peak - Naples: Two days exploring the archaeological wonders of Herculaneum and the vibrant, chaotic energy of southern ItalyтАЩs largest city - Puglia: Six days discovering the lesser-known heel of ItalyтАЩs boot, with its whitewashed hill towns, coastal beauty, and distinctive regional character

text:
Trip Context

Arrival (June 5)
Arriving from Pisa by train (~1 hour), checking into hotel, afternoon orientation walk. This is our soft landing after overnight flight - gentle exploration, early dinner, rest.


Full Days (June 6-9)
Four complete days dedicated to FlorenceтАЩs artistic and cultural treasures:
Day 1 (June 6): Duomo Complex, Uffizi Gallery Day 2 (June 7): Accademia (David), Medici sites Day 3 (June 8): Bargello, Santa Croce, markets Day 4 (June 9): Palazzo Pitti, Boboli Gardens, final museums, shopping

title: Florence Overview
text:
Florence at a Glance

The Renaissance Happened Here
1400-1600: Florence exploded with artistic and intellectual genius, producing: - Michelangelo, Leonardo da Vinci, Botticelli, Raphael (artists) - Dante, Machiavelli, Galileo (writers, thinkers) - The Medici family (patrons who funded it all) - BrunelleschiтАЩs architectural innovations - Linear perspective, humanist philosophy, modern science
Result: The Renaissance spread from Florence to transform Europe and, eventually, the world.


The Medici Family
Why They Matter: For 300 years (1434-1737), this banking family ruled Florence and bankrolled the Renaissance.
What They Left: - Funded construction of the DuomoтАЩs dome - Commissioned countless masterpieces - Built Palazzo Pitti, Palazzo Vecchio - Established the Uffizi Gallery - Patronized Michelangelo, Leonardo, Botticelli - Founded the Medici Chapels (their mausoleum)
Without the Medici: No Sistine Chapel ceiling (they commissioned Michelangelo), no Birth of Venus, no David statue.

title: Florence Overview
text:
Major Attractions

Art Museums (Priority Bookings Required)
Uffizi Gallery - WorldтАЩs finest Renaissance art collection - BotticelliтАЩs Birth of Venus and Primavera - Leonardo, Michelangelo, Raphael, Caravaggio - Book 2-3 months ahead
Galleria dellтАЩAccademia - MichelangeloтАЩs David - Unfinished Prisoners sculptures - Book 2-3 months ahead
Bargello Museum - Best sculpture collection - DonatelloтАЩs bronze David - Less crowded than Uffizi
Palazzo Pitti - Palatine Gallery (Raphael, Titian) - Royal Apartments - Boboli Gardens


Churches & Architecture
Duomo Complex - Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore - BrunelleschiтАЩs Dome (463-step climb, book ahead!) - GiottoтАЩs Bell Tower (Campanile) - Baptistery with GhibertiтАЩs тАЬGates of ParadiseтАЭ
Santa Croce - Franciscan church - Tombs of Michelangelo, Galileo, Machiavelli - Giotto frescoes
Medici Chapels - MichelangeloтАЩs sculptural tombs - Opulent Chapel of Princes
San Lorenzo - Medici family church - Laurentian Library (Michelangelo staircase)
San Marco - Fra Angelico frescoes - Former monastery


Iconic Sites
Ponte Vecchio - Medieval bridge with jewelry shops - Only bridge Nazis didnтАЩt destroy in WWII - Sunset views of Arno River
Piazza della Signoria - FlorenceтАЩs civic square since Middle Ages - Palazzo Vecchio (city hall) - Copy of David (original was here) - Loggia dei Lanzi (outdoor sculpture gallery)
Piazzale Michelangelo - Hilltop overlook with panoramic Florence views - Sunset essential - Copy of David statue

title: Florence Overview
text:
Neighborhoods

Centro Storico (Historic Center)
Duomo Area - Tourist epicenter - Cathedral, baptistery, museums - Crowded but must-see - Avoid staying here (too touristy, noisy)
Santa Maria Novella (Near train station) - Convenient for arrivals/departures - Good hotels and restaurants - Church of Santa Maria Novella (beautiful facade) - Pharmacy of Santa Maria Novella (oldest in Europe, 1612)


San Lorenzo / Mercato Centrale

Local character, less touristy
Mercato Centrale (food market - amazing!)
Leather market stalls
Near Medici Chapels and San Lorenzo church
Great place to stay



Oltrarno (Across the River)

тАЬOther side of the ArnoтАЭ
Artisan workshops, antique shops
Palazzo Pitti and Boboli Gardens
Excellent local restaurants
Quieter at night
Santo Spirito square (evening aperitivo scene)



Santa Croce

Vibrant piazza with nightlife
Santa Croce church
Mix of local and tourist
Walking distance to everything

title: Florence Overview
text:
Getting Around

Walking City
Florence is MADE for walking: - Historic center is compact (1 mile across) - Most sights within 20-minute walk - Pedestrian zones (no cars)
Expect: - 5-10 miles of walking per day - Cobblestone streets (wear good shoes!) - Sore feet (totally worth it)


Public Transportation
Buses: - ATAF orange buses - тВм1.50 for 90 minutes - Buy tickets at tabacchi (tobacco) shops or machines - Validate on bus (yellow machines)
Not Really Needed: - Historic center is walkable - Exception: Piazzale Michelangelo (#12 or #13 bus)


Taxis

White cars with тАЬTAXIтАЭ sign
Expensive by U.S. standards (тВм10-15 for short rides)
Use for: airport, late night, heavy bags
App: it Taxi or Free Now

Most close Mondays - Plan accordingly!

title: Florence Overview
text:
Tips for Florence

Beat the Crowds
Book early entry (8:15 AM): - Uffizi - Accademia - Duomo dome climb
Late afternoon (after 4 PM): - Smaller museums - Churches
Avoid weekends if possible (but we canтАЩt control our dates)


Museum Strategy
DonтАЩt try to see everything: - Pick 2-3 must-see pieces per museum - Focus on quality, not quantity - Take breaks (museum fatigue is real)
Pace Yourself: - Max 2 major museums per day - Alternate museum days with outdoor days - Rest when tired (thereтАЩs always tomorrow)


Dress Code
Churches: - Shoulders and knees covered - Bring scarf to cover up - They WILL turn you away if improperly dressed
Museums: - No restrictions (casual fine)


Best Times
Morning (8-10 AM): - Quiet streets - Locals at caf├йs - Museums less crowded
Late Afternoon (4-7 PM): - Aperitivo hour (drink + free snacks) - Museums emptying out - Good light for photos
Evening (8-10 PM): - Dinner time - Piazzas come alive - Street performers - Gelato walks

title: Puglia Overview
text:
About Puglia
La Puglia - The heel of ItalyтАЩs boot, stretching 250 miles down the Adriatic coast. ItalyтАЩs best-kept secret: stunning coastline, unique architecture, incredible food, authentic culture, and far fewer tourists than Tuscany or the Amalfi Coast.
Our Time Here: 6 days (June 14-19) on organized tour
Why Puglia? - UNESCO trulli houses (unique cone-shaped buildings) - LecceтАЩs baroque architecture (тАЬFlorence of the SouthтАЭ) - Dramatic Adriatic coastline - Authentic southern Italian culture - Exceptional food and wine - Whitewashed hill towns - Off the beaten tourist path

text:

title: Puglia Overview
text:
Puglia at a Glance

Geography
The Heel of the Boot: - 250-mile long region along Adriatic Sea - Mostly flat (unlike mountainous northern Italy) - Endless olive groves (40% of ItalyтАЩs olive oil production) - Dramatic limestone coastline - Hot, dry climate (similar to Greece)


History
Ancient Crossroads: - Greek colonies (Magna Graecia, 8th century BC) - Roman conquest and agricultural development - Byzantine influence (Eastern Orthodox traditions) - Norman, Swabian, Spanish rule (Frederick II built Castel del Monte) - Always a bridge between East and West
Result: Unique culture blending Italian, Greek, Byzantine, and Norman influences.


Character
Why It Feels Different: - Less touristy than northern Italy - More Greek/Mediterranean than typically Italian - Rural, agricultural traditions still strong - Slower pace of life - Locals genuinely surprised and pleased to see visitors - Pride in regional identity (Pugliese first, Italian second)

title: Puglia Overview
text:
Expected Tour Highlights
Based on typical Puglia tours, we expect to visit:

Alberobello тнР UNESCO World Heritage
The Trulli Capital
What Are Trulli? - Cone-shaped stone buildings unique to Puglia - Built without mortar (dry stone construction) - Whitewashed walls, conical gray stone roofs - Originally built to avoid taxes (could be quickly dismantled when tax collectors came) - Now UNESCO protected
What to See: - Rione Monti (1,000+ trulli, densest concentration) - Rione Aia Piccola (more residential, fewer tourists) - Trullo Sovrano (two-story trullo, now museum) - Church of SantтАЩAntonio (trullo-style church)
Experience: - Walk through trulli streets - Visit shops in converted trulli - Possible overnight stay IN a trullo - Wine tasting (some trulli are wineries)


Lecce тнР тАЬFlorence of the SouthтАЭ
Baroque Masterpiece
Why ItтАЩs Special: - 17th-century baroque architecture in golden limestone - Incredibly ornate churches and palaces - Compact, walkable historic center - Less crowded than Florence but equally beautiful
What to See: - Piazza del Duomo - One of ItalyтАЩs most beautiful squares - Basilica di Santa Croce - Baroque facade covered in sculptures - Roman Amphitheater - 2nd century AD, still partially excavated - Castello Carlo V - 16th-century fortress - Artisan workshops (papier-m├вch├й, ceramics)
Experience: - Baroque architecture walking tour - Pasticciotto (custard-filled pastry) at Caff├и Alvino - Evening passeggiata (stroll) in piazzas


Polignano a Mare тнР Coastal Drama
Cliffside Beauty
Setting: - Town perched on 20-meter limestone cliffs - Dramatic views over Adriatic Sea - Sea caves below - Former fishing village, now charming resort town
What to See: - Historic Center - Narrow streets, whitewashed buildings - Lama Monachile Beach - Dramatic beach between cliffs - Red Bull Cliff Diving - Location of annual competition - Grotta Palazzese - Restaurant inside sea cave (very expensive)
Experience: - Cliff-top walk with sea views - Swimming (if weather permits) - Seafood lunch overlooking Adriatic - Gelato in historic center


Ostuni - тАЬLa Citt├а BiancaтАЭ (The White City)
Hilltop Marvel
Appearance: - Whitewashed buildings cascading down hillside - Labyrinth of narrow streets and staircases - Cathedral at the summit - Surrounded by olive groves stretching to sea
What to See: - Historic Center - Explore maze of white streets - Cathedral of Ostuni - Gothic-Romanesque facade - Panoramic Views - Over olive groves to Adriatic - Medieval Walls - Partially intact defensive walls
Experience: - Getting lost in the white maze (intentionally!) - Olive oil tasting - Photography (stunning at any time of day) - Aperitivo with a view


Other Possible Stops
Tours often include some of these:
Matera (Technically Basilicata, but often included) - Sassi di Matera - Ancient cave dwellings, UNESCO site - Continuously inhabited for 9,000 years - Used in films (The Passion of the Christ, No Time to Die)
Castel del Monte тнР UNESCO - Frederick IIтАЩs mysterious octagonal castle (1240) - Perfect mathematical proportions - Isolated hilltop location - Unknown original purpose
Otranto - Easternmost town in Italy - Byzantine mosaics in cathedral - Turquoise Adriatic waters - Albania visible on clear days
Locorotondo - Circular hilltop town - White houses, narrow streets - Valle dтАЩItria views
Monopoli - Fishing port with old town - Less touristy than Polignano - Authentic harbor life
Coastal Areas - Beaches along Adriatic coast - Sea caves and grottoes - Swimming opportunities

title: Puglia Overview
text:
Puglia Food & Wine

Regional Specialties
Pasta: - Orecchiette - тАЬLittle earsтАЭ shaped pasta (PugliaтАЩs signature) - Orecchiette con cime di rapa (with turnip greens, garlic, anchovies) - Cavatelli with tomato sauce
Seafood: - Fresh from Adriatic daily - Grilled fish, octopus, mussels - Sea urchin (ricci di mare) - Riso, patate e cozze (rice, potatoes, mussels - baked)
Meat: - Bombette - Meat rolls stuffed with cheese - Gnummareddi - Lamb offal rolls - Grilled meats (Pugliese love their BBQ)
Cheese: - Burrata - Creamy mozzarella (INVENTED in Puglia!) - Burrata di Andria (the original) - Caciocavallo - Ricotta forte (aged, strong flavor)
Bread & Baked Goods: - Focaccia Barese - With tomatoes and olives - Friselle - Hard bread rings (soak in water, top with tomatoes) - Panzerotti - Fried half-moon calzones - Pasticciotto - Custard-filled pastry (Lecce specialty) - Rustico Leccese - Puff pastry with mozzarella and b├йchamel


Wine
Puglia = ItalyтАЩs Wine Cellar (produces more wine than any other region)
Red Wines: - Primitivo - Full-bodied, fruity (related to California Zinfandel) - Negroamaro - тАЬBlack bitter,тАЭ smooth, cherry notes - Primitivo di Manduria - DOC designation, premium - Salice Salentino - Blend, very drinkable
White Wines: - Verdeca - Crisp, citrusy - Locorotondo - Fresh, light - Fiano Minutolo - Aromatic
Ros├й: - Rosato del Salento - Perfect with seafood


Olive Oil
Puglia produces 40% of ItalyтАЩs olive oil
What to Know: - Ancient olive trees (some 1,000+ years old) - DOP designations (Terra di Bari, Dauno, etc.) - Tastings often included in tours - Buy bottles to bring home (pack carefully!)

title: Puglia Overview
text:
Practical Information

When WeтАЩre There
June 14-19: Early summer - Weather: High 80s┬░F (28-30┬░C), sunny, dry - Coastal breezes make it more comfortable than Naples - Long days (sunset ~8:30 PM) - Peak season starting (but less touristy than Tuscany)


Getting Around
Tour Transportation: - Likely by tour bus or van - All inter-town transport included - May rotate accommodations or stay in one base
Within Towns: - Walking (most historic centers pedestrianized) - Hills and stairs (Ostuni especially) - Cobblestones (again, good shoes!)


Language
Pugliese Dialect: - Very different from standard Italian - Greek and Albanian influences - Locals speak Italian with tourists - English less common than Florence/Rome
Useful: - тАЬBuongiornoтАЭ goes a long way - Attempt Italian - locals appreciate effort - Many tour guides bilingual


Money
Budget TBD (depends on tour package): - Tour likely includes: accommodations, some meals, transportation, guides - YouтАЩll pay for: additional meals, drinks, souvenirs, personal expenses
Expected Daily: тВм30-50 for meals not included, wine, gelato, etc.


Safety
Very Safe: - Low crime - Smaller towns feel even safer than cities - Watch belongings in tourist areas (standard precaution)

text:
Arriving in Pisa: Stay or Go?
You have two good options when landing at Pisa Galileo Galilei Airport (PSA):

Option A: Head Straight to Florence (Recommended)







Step
Details




From Airport
Take the PisaMover shuttle (8 min, automated) to Pisa Centrale station


Train
Direct Regionale to Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN)


Duration
50тАУ70 minutes depending on service


Frequency
Every 30 minutes, ~52 trains per day

тВм10тАУтВм12 one way (fixed price, no advance booking needed)

This is the most efficient option. YouтАЩll be in Florence within 2 hours of clearing customs. The train drops you at SMN station, right in the historic center.


Option B: Overnight in Pisa
If you arrive late or want to ease into the trip, spending one night in Pisa is pleasant. The city is charming beyond the Leaning Tower:
Why Stay: - Piazza dei Miracoli (with the Tower, Cathedral, and Baptistery) is genuinely spectacular - Arno riverfront at sunset is lovely - See PisaтАЩs highlights in a leisurely evening and morning - Reduce travel stress after long flight
Where to Stay: - Stay near the train station or along the Arno тАФ not near the Tower (touristy and overpriced) - Hotel Bologna or Royal Victoria are good mid-range picks
What to See: - Leaning Tower and Piazza dei Miracoli complex (allow 2-3 hours) - Walk along the Arno - Quiet piazzas away from the tourist center

title: Flights to Italy
text:
Booking Strategy

When to Book
Timeline: - 2-3 months ahead: Best balance of price and availability - Too early: Prices often havenтАЩt dropped to competitive levels - Too late: Limited availability, higher prices
For our June 4 departure: Book by late March/early April 2026


Booking Checklist

Compare prices on Google Flights, airline sites, and SkyLux
Check baggage allowances (especially for souvenirs on return)
Select seats early (aisle vs.┬аwindow preference)
Add to calendar with confirmation numbers
Print or download boarding passes 24 hours before
Notify credit cards of international travel dates
Check passport validity (must be valid 6 months beyond return)



Seat Selection Tips
Transatlantic Leg: - Business/First: Window for sleep, aisle for movement - Economy: Aisle for long-haul comfort and bathroom access - Avoid seats near lavatories and galleys (noise) - Emergency exit rows: More legroom but no under-seat storage during takeoff/landing
European Connection: - Usually shorter flights (1-2 hours) - Less critical but still book early for best selection

Carry-On
Standard: 1 carry-on + 1 personal item
Pack in Carry-On: - Medications - Valuables (jewelry, electronics) - Change of clothes (in case checked bag delayed) - Travel documents - Essential toiletries (3.4 oz / 100ml limit)

title: Museums & Attractions
text:
Florence Museums

Uffizi Gallery тнР PRIORITY BOOKING
Why Visit: Home to the worldтАЩs finest Renaissance art collection
Masterpieces: - BotticelliтАЩs Birth of Venus and Primavera - Leonardo da VinciтАЩs Annunciation - MichelangeloтАЩs Doni Tondo - CaravaggioтАЩs Medusa - Raphael, Titian, and countless others
Practical Information: - Booking: ESSENTIAL - Book 2-3 months ahead - Website: uffizi.it - Price: тВм20-тВм25 (includes temporary exhibitions) - Time Needed: 2.5-4 hours - Best Time: First entry (8:15 AM) or late afternoon - Closed: Mondays
Tips: - Book timed entry slot - Arrive 15 min early with printed confirmation - Start on 2nd floor, work chronologically - DonтАЩt miss Botticelli rooms (10-14) - Caf├й terrace has Duomo views



Galleria dellтАЩAccademia (David) тнР PRIORITY BOOKING
Why Visit: MichelangeloтАЩs David statue
Highlights: - David - The iconic 17-foot marble masterpiece - MichelangeloтАЩs Prisoners (unfinished sculptures showing his process) - Plaster models and studies - Musical instrument collection
Practical Information: - Booking: ESSENTIAL - Sells out weeks ahead - Website: galleriaaccademiafirenze.it - Price: тВм12-тВм16 - Time Needed: 1-1.5 hours - Best Time: First entry (8:15 AM) to see David with fewer crowds - Closed: Mondays
Tips: - Smaller museum than Uffizi - quicker visit - David is in the Tribune gallery (canтАЩt miss it) - Get there early - crowds gather by mid-morning - Photography allowed (no flash)



Duomo Complex (Cathedral, Dome, Baptistery, Bell Tower)
WhatтАЩs Included: - Cathedral (free entry to nave) - BrunelleschiтАЩs Dome climb - GiottoтАЩs Bell Tower (Campanile) - Baptistery (GhibertiтАЩs тАЬGates of ParadiseтАЭ) - Opera Museum - Crypt
Booking: - Dome Climb: MUST BOOK - Limited capacity, timed entry - Website: duomo.firenze.it - Price: тВм30 (combo ticket for all sites, valid 3 days) - Dome Climb: 463 steps, no elevator, narrow passages - Time: 2-3 hours for all sites - Closed: Sundays (religious services)
Dome Climb Tips: - Book earliest available time - Not for claustrophobic or mobility-limited - Incredible 360┬░ Florence views from top - See VasariтАЩs frescoes up close



Bargello Museum
Why Visit: WorldтАЩs best Renaissance sculpture collection
Highlights: - DonatelloтАЩs bronze David (first nude sculpture since antiquity) - MichelangeloтАЩs early works - Della Robbia ceramics - Verrocchio sculptures
Practical Information: - Booking: Recommended but not always essential - Website: bargellomusei.beniculturali.it - Price: тВм9-тВм11 - Time Needed: 1.5-2 hours - Best Time: Afternoons (less crowded) - Closed: 2nd and 4th Mondays
Tips: - Often overlooked - fewer crowds - Start on ground floor, work up - DonтАЩt miss Donatello room (first floor)



Medici Chapels
Why Visit: MichelangeloтАЩs sculptural tombs for the Medici family
Highlights: - Tomb of Lorenzo deтАЩ Medici with Dawn and Dusk - Tomb of Giuliano deтАЩ Medici with Day and Night - Cappella dei Principi (Chapel of Princes) - opulent marble - Medici family tombs
Practical Information: - Booking: Recommended - Website: bargellomusei.beniculturali.it - Price: тВм9 - Time Needed: 45-60 minutes - Closed: Mondays



Palazzo Pitti & Boboli Gardens
WhatтАЩs Included: - Palatine Gallery (Raphael, Titian paintings) - Royal Apartments - Modern Art Gallery - Costume Gallery - Boboli Gardens (16th-century formal gardens)
Practical Information: - Booking: Recommended for peak times - Website: uffizi.it - Price: тВм16 (palace + gardens) - Time Needed: 2-3 hours (more if leisurely garden stroll) - Best Time: Morning for palace, afternoon for gardens
Tips: - Gardens are extensive - wear comfortable shoes - Great escape from museum crowds - Caf├й in gardens for break



Santa Croce
Why Visit: Franciscan church with tombs of famous Florentines
Highlights: - Tombs of Michelangelo, Galileo, Machiavelli, Rossini - Giotto frescoes (Bardi and Peruzzi Chapels) - Pazzi Chapel by Brunelleschi - Leather school (free to enter from side)
Practical Information: - Booking: Not required - Price: тВм8 - Time Needed: 45-60 minutes - Dress Code: Shoulders and knees covered



Firenze Card
What Is It: тВм85 pass valid 72 hours
Includes: - Uffizi, Accademia, Palazzo Pitti, Bargello, Medici Chapels - 60+ museums and sites - Skip-the-line access (reserve time slots)
Worth It? - YES if visiting 4+ major museums in 3 days - NO if leisurely pace (2-3 museums)
Calculate: тВм20 (Uffizi) + тВм16 (Accademia) + тВм16 (Pitti) + тВм9 (Bargello) = тВм61 - Card pays for itself with 4 museums - Bonus: Skip lines, flexibility
Buy: firenzecard.it

title: Museums & Attractions
text:
Naples Museums

MANN (Museo Archeologico Nazionale di Napoli) тнР PRIORITY
Why Visit: WorldтАЩs finest Roman artifacts, most from Pompeii & Herculaneum
Collections: - Pompeii and Herculaneum artifacts - Farnese Collection (massive Roman sculptures) - Alexander Mosaic (from Pompeii) - Secret Cabinet (erotic Roman art) - Egyptian collection - Coin and medal collection
Practical Information: - Booking: Recommended (can buy at door but may have lines) - Website: museoarcheologiconapoli.it - Price: тВм18 - Time Needed: 2-3 hours (could spend full day) - Best Time: Morning - Closed: Tuesdays
Tips: - Go AFTER Herculaneum visit (context makes artifacts meaningful) - DonтАЩt miss Secret Cabinet (room VII) - Huge museum - pace yourself - Caf├й for breaks



Cappella Sansevero тнР MUST SEE
Why Visit: The Veiled Christ - one of the worldтАЩs most astonishing sculptures
Highlights: - Veiled Christ by Giuseppe Sanmartino (1753) - marble veil appears translucent - Disillusion by Francesco Queirolo - fishermanтАЩs net carved in marble - Modesty by Antonio Corradini - Ceiling fresco Glory of Paradise
Practical Information: - Booking: ESSENTIAL - Timed entry, very small chapel - Website: museosansevero.it - Price: тВм9 - Time Needed: 30-45 minutes - Photography: NOT allowed - Closed: Tuesdays
Tips: - Book earliest slot available - Tiny chapel - gets very crowded - Stand directly under the Veiled Christ - Worth the booking hassle - unforgettable



Other Naples Sites
Napoli Sotterranea (Underground Naples) - 40 meters below street level - Ancient Roman theater, WWII shelters - Greek aqueducts - Guided tours only - Book at napolisotterranea.org
Castel Nuovo (Maschio Angioino) - Medieval castle - Civic Museum - Great views

DonтАЩt Over-Schedule: - Max 2 major museums per day - Morning museum + afternoon lighter activity - Build in rest/wandering time

At the Museum

Inside: - Ask for museum map - Identify тАЬmust-seeтАЭ pieces first - Take breaks (museum fatigue is real) - Photography rules vary (usually no flash, sometimes no photos)
Crowds: - Worst: 11 AM - 2 PM - Best: First entry (8:15 AM) or after 4 PM - Avoid weekends if possible

title: Organized Tours
text:
Herculaneum & Vesuvius Tour (June 12)

Status: Confirmed тАФ Details Pending
The Herculaneum & Vesuvius tour is confirmed for June 12, 2026. This section will be updated with booking reference, provider name, meeting point, and exact timing once the confirmation file is added to the reservations folder.


Why Herculaneum?
Herculaneum is the more intimate, better-preserved, and less crowded alternative to Pompeii. While Pompeii was buried under ash, Herculaneum was sealed under a 20-meter lava flow, which preserved:

Upper floors of buildings
Wooden furniture and architectural elements
Carbonized food
Intact frescoes
Even organic materials like fabrics

Most Powerful Moment: The Boat Houses, where 300 victims were discovered huddled together. Skeletons are still visible - the most emotionally powerful archaeological site in Italy.


Getting to Herculaneum
From Naples: - Circumvesuviana train from Napoli Centrale (Garibaldi station, lower level) - Direction: Sorrento - Exit at: Ercolano Scavi station - Duration: ~20 minutes - Price: ~тВм2.80 - Then: 10-minute downhill walk to entrance
Note: Tours often include transportation from Naples hotel, eliminating need to navigate Circumvesuviana.


What YouтАЩll See at Herculaneum
The Boat Houses - 300 victims discovered huddled together seeking escape by sea - Skeletons still visible in original positions - Most emotionally powerful moment in archaeology - Understanding of the eruptionтАЩs human toll
House of Neptune and Amphitrite - One of the most vivid Roman mosaics in existence - Brilliant blue and gold glass tesserae - Perfectly preserved dining area (triclinium) - Shows wealth and taste of Roman elite
Casa dei Cervi (House of the Stags) - Elite villa with spectacular frescoes - Garden and former sea-view terrace - Marble sculptures of stags being attacked by dogs - Multi-level structure showing urban Roman architecture
The Thermopolium - Ancient Roman тАЬfast foodтАЭ counter - Preserved terracotta jars (dolia) for food storage - Shows daily life of common Romans - Still has the marble counter and serving area
Sacello degli Augustali - Spectacular frescoes of Hercules (cityтАЩs legendary founder) - PriestsтАЩ building with elaborate decoration - Best-preserved Roman wall paintings
Preserved Organic Materials - Wooden doors, beds, shelving - BabyтАЩs cradle - Rope - Food (carbonized bread, nuts, dates) - Items completely destroyed at Pompeii


Tour Options
A guided tour is strongly recommended. The difference between wandering alone and having an archaeologist explain the details is enormous.


Option 1: Small Group - Askos Tours (Best Value)
Duration: 2 hours Group Size: Max 20 people (often smaller in practice) Guide: Licensed archaeologist Included: Skip-the-line tickets + guided tour Price: ~тВм50-тВм65 per person Rating: 4.5/5 on Viator (1,160+ reviews)
Booking: Viator.com - Search тАЬHerculaneum Small Group TourтАЭ
Pros: - Excellent value - Professional archaeologist guides - Skip ticket lines - Small enough for personal attention - Highly rated
Cons: - Fixed departure time - Shared with other travelers - Limited ability to linger at specific areas



Option 2: Private Archaeologist - Askos Tours
Duration: 2 hours, at your pace Group Size: Private (just our party) Guide: Licensed archaeologist Included: Skip-the-line tickets + personalized tour Price: ~тВм180-тВм250 total (not per person) Booking: AskOsTours.com/herculaneum
Pros: - Go at your own pace - Ask unlimited questions - Customize focus areas - Spend more/less time as desired - Excellent value for 2+ people
Cons: - Higher cost for solo travelers - Need to coordinate meeting time
Recommendation: This is our top choice for personalized experience without high cost.



Option 3: Private Both Sites - LivTours (Gold Standard)
Duration: 6-7 hours (full day) Included: Both Pompeii + Herculaneum, private chauffeur transfer, all tickets Guide: Expert archaeologist Transportation: Private driver between sites and from/to Naples hotel Price: ~тВм400-тВм600 total for 2 people Rating: 4.8/5 across platforms Booking: LivTours.com
Pros: - See both major sites in one day - No logistics worries (driver handles everything) - VIP experience - Comprehensive archaeological day - Hotel pickup/drop-off
Cons: - Full day commitment (can be exhausting) - Higher cost - Pompeii can be overwhelming in summer heat - May feel rushed if trying to see both thoroughly
Best For: Those who want to see both Pompeii and Herculaneum and prefer hassle-free logistics.



Option 4: Full Day Private Tour with Vesuvius
Duration: 8 hours Included: Hotel pickup, private driver, Herculaneum + Vesuvius crater hike + Pompeii (optional) Entrance Fees: Included Guide: Can upgrade to include archaeologist throughout or just at ruins Price: ~тВм350-тВм550 total for 2 people (guide upgrade extra) Booking: Viator.com - Search тАЬHerculaneum Vesuvius Pompeii PrivateтАЭ
Itinerary: - Morning: Herculaneum ruins (2 hours) - Midday: Drive up Mt. Vesuvius, hike to crater rim (1-1.5 hours) - Afternoon: Optional Pompeii or return to Naples
Pros: - Combines archaeology with volcano experience - See the source of the destruction - Incredible views from Vesuvius crater - Flexible itinerary
Cons: - Long day - Vesuvius hike requires moderate fitness (steep path) - CanтАЩt go deep into all sites if doing all three
Best For: Those who want to understand the full Vesuvius story and are physically able to hike.




Our Recommendation
For Herculaneum Only: Askos Private Tour (Option 2) - тВм180-250 for personalized 2-hour experience with archaeologist
If Adding Vesuvius: Full Day Private (Option 4) - тВм350-550 for Herculaneum + Vesuvius crater hike
If Seeing Both Ancient Sites: LivTours (Option 3) - тВм400-600 for comprehensive Pompeii + Herculaneum with all logistics
Budget Option: Askos Small Group (Option 1) - тВм50-65 per person for excellent guided experience



Booking Checklist

Decide on tour option (Herculaneum only vs.┬аcombo)
Book 2-4 weeks in advance (tours fill up)
Confirm meeting point (hotel pickup vs.┬аmeet at site)
Note guideтАЩs name and contact number
Add to calendar with confirmation number
Plan appropriate clothing (comfortable shoes, sun protection)
Bring water bottle
Charge camera/phone



What to Bring
Essential: - Comfortable walking shoes (uneven ancient streets) - Sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen) - Water bottle (stays hydrated in June heat) - Camera with charged battery
Recommended: - Small backpack or crossbody bag - Cash for tips (тВм5-10 for guide, optional but appreciated) - Notebook if you want to take notes - Light layers (some buildings cooler than outside)
Not Needed: - Large bags (bag check available but cumbersome) - Umbrellas (ruins partially exposed to elements)

text:
Puglia Regional Tour (June 14-19)

Status: Tour Being Finalized
WeтАЩre currently planning a 6-day organized tour of Puglia from June 14-19, 2026. This section will be updated once tour details are confirmed.


What We Know
Dates: June 14-19, 2026 (6 days / 5 nights) Region: Puglia (southeastern Italy, the тАЬheel of the bootтАЭ) Starting Point: Brindisi (arriving June 13 from Naples) Tour Type: Organized group or semi-private tour Focus: Culture, architecture, food, wine, coastal beauty


Expected Highlights
Based on typical Puglia tours, we expect to visit:
Alberobello - UNESCO World Heritage trulli houses - Unique cone-shaped stone buildings - Trulli wine tasting - Overnight stay possible
Lecce - тАЬFlorence of the SouthтАЭ - Baroque architecture in golden limestone - Piazza del Duomo - Roman amphitheater - Artisan workshops
Polignano a Mare - Dramatic cliffside coastal town - Beaches and sea caves - Red Bull Cliff Diving location - Fresh seafood restaurants
Ostuni - La Citt├а Bianca (тАЬThe White CityтАЭ) - Hilltop old town with whitewashed buildings - Panoramic views over olive groves - Medieval character
Other Possible Stops: - Matera (technically Basilicata, but often included) - Ancient cave dwellings - Castel del Monte - Frederick IIтАЩs mysterious octagonal castle - Otranto - Easternmost town in Italy, beautiful cathedral - Locorotondo - Circular hilltop town - Cisternino - Another white hilltop town - Coastal areas and beaches


Puglia Experiences
Culinary: - Orecchiette pasta (shaped like тАЬlittle earsтАЭ) - Burrata cheese (originated in Puglia) - Focaccia Barese - Fresh seafood - Primitivo and Negroamaro wines - Olive oil tastings (Puglia produces 40% of ItalyтАЩs olive oil)
Cultural: - Trullo architecture (unique to Puglia) - Baroque churches and piazzas - Masseria (fortified farmhouse) stays - Traditional folk music and dance - Artisan workshops (ceramics, paper mache)


Tour Logistics (To Be Confirmed)
Accommodation: - Likely included in tour package - May rotate between towns or stay in one base - Possible masseria (traditional farmhouse) experience
Meals: - Some meals likely included - Emphasis on regional cuisine - Wine tastings probable
Transportation: - Tour bus or van - All inter-city transport within Puglia included - Hotel to hotel transfers

Group size (small group vs.┬аprivate)

title: Organized Tours
text:
Tour Booking Tips (General)

When to Book
Herculaneum Tours: - Book 2-4 weeks in advance - Popular times (summer weekends) book faster - Weekday mornings less crowded
Puglia Tours: - Book 2-3 months in advance - Multi-day tours require more planning - Summer is peak season


What to Ask Tour Operators

Group size (smaller is better for archaeology sites)
Guide credentials (archaeologist vs.┬аgeneral guide)
Cancellation policy
WhatтАЩs included (tickets, transportation, meals)
Meeting point and time
Physical difficulty level
Language (English-speaking guide confirmed?)



Red Flags to Avoid

Extremely cheap prices (often crowded mega-buses)
Vague itineraries
No reviews or very few reviews
High-pressure booking tactics
No clear cancellation policy



Payment & Confirmation

Book through reputable platforms (Viator, TripAdvisor, direct with operator)
Get written confirmation email
Save booking number and emergency contact
Check credit card for proper charge
Screenshot confirmation for offline access
